attempts.insight: The unique-gluing form is Mathlib-equivalent to the OpensLeCover form (the two arrows are framework lemmas: `isSheaf_of_isSheafUniqueGluing_types` and `IsSheaf.isSheafOpensLeCover`, both already in Mathlib with no project-side adapters). This pivot routes around — does NOT close — the basis-to-opens descent gap (still documented at chapter L51 `[gap]`). The next prover iter still has to prove the substantive content (compatibility ⇒ gluing on the structure-sheaf side via the universal `KaehlerDifferential` construction + `span_range_derivation` uniqueness). The Bar A/B/C taxonomy the plan agent specified is binary on 'did Sub-lemma A or Sub-lemma B close?'; this iter answers neither (Bar C by strict reading) but also does not exhibit the helper-explosion failure mode (only ONE new top-level helper, not two). Net classification: between Bar B and Bar C — better than helper-explosion, worse than substantive sub-lemma closure.

next_steps: Iter-114 prover lane on `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheafUniqueGluing_type` (L168 of Differentials.lean). Three-step recipe documented in the new helper's docstring (L148–L165): (1) compatibility ⇒ gluing on the structure-sheaf side via the universal derivation `d : O_X → Ω_{X/S}` + `X.ringCatSheaf.existsUnique_gluing`; (2) lift back via `CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.D` + `ModuleCat.Derivation.desc`; (3) uniqueness via `KaehlerDifferential.span_range_derivation` + `eq_of_locally_eq`.
